data.body:
Goal:
Refactor `kno doctor` so that by default it iterates over every accessible registered project (from `kno project list`) and runs all applicable checks against each, with per-project output grouping. `--fix` applies per-project fixes. Skip projects whose paths don't exist; warn (don't fail) on ones that can't be opened.

Motivation: today doctor only checks the cwd repo. When a user has N registered projects that all need the same fix (e.g., skills updated, hooks reinstalled, schema migrated), they currently have to `cd` into each one. That scales badly.

Verification:
1. With 3 registered projects on disk, `kno doctor` prints sections for each, one line per check per project.
2. With a registered project whose path was deleted, doctor silently skips it (or lists it as "unreachable" without failing).
3. `kno doctor --fix` applied to a missing-skills failure in 3 projects installs skills in all 3 in one run.
4. Performance: doctor on 20 projects completes in under 5 seconds on a warm machine.
5. Output format is readable both in tty mode and `--json` mode.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the semantics of individual checks — just run them against each project.
- Checks that are inherently global (e.g., binary version) run once, not N times.
- `kno project list` currently contains ~200 stale e2e/test fixtures; this knot assumes the user has pruned them or a separate cleanup knot runs first. Pair with a follow-up knot: "Prune stale project registrations in kno project list."
- Related knot 8aba shipped a cold-tier rebalance check; when this refactor lands, ensure that check also runs cross-project.
data.title: Make kno doctor check all registered projects by default
